title: karpatiosorbus veszpremensis
intro: Karpatiosorbus veszpremensis, also known as the Hungarian mountain ash, is a plant species in the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Carpathian Mountains in Eastern Europe, used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's berries and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to their high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. Karpatiosorbus veszpremensis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits and medicinal properties.
